scenario: CoinGecko's 1TB+ 'prices' table storing 8+ years of hourly cryptocurrency price data had grown so large that queries took over 30 seconds on average. Daily IOPS alerts were being breached, Apdex scores were dropping, and replica lag was becoming a persistent problem under normal request loads.
challenge: A 1TB+ PostgreSQL table with 8+ years of hourly crypto price data had degraded to the point where queries timed out, IOPS limits were breached daily, and simply adding more indexes wasn't working—especially because the table used JSONB columns with currency-dependent keys that made traditional indexing impractical.
solution: Implemented monthly RANGE partitioning by timestamp after analyzing query patterns (most queries hit only 1-4 months of data). Used PostgreSQL's declarative partitioning with a creative Foreign Data Wrapper approach to migrate 1.2TB of data without warming the production cache—spinning up a separate production-grade database, pre-warming the data there, and streaming it via FDW into the production partitioned table.
outcome: P99 response time dropped 86% from 4.13s to 578ms. IOPS reduced by 20% across all replicas. Query performance improved 6-8x. Table warm-up time dropped from 10 hours to 3 hours. The API became significantly more stable and resilient during traffic spikes.
lesson: Partitioning is not a silver bullet—one query that lacked a lower date bound (WHERE created_at > ? without upper limit) performed WORSE after partitioning because it scanned all partitions including empty future ones. The team had to rewrite queries to always include explicit range bounds. They also learned that creating all future partitions upfront can backfire if queries don't bound both sides of the range.
sourceTitle: Scaling PostgreSQL Performance with Table Partitioning
reason: Directly matches the topic (date-range partitioned table with slow queries). Has the 'plot twist' of a query getting WORSE after partitioning. Specific measurable outcomes (86% latency reduction, 20% IOPS savings). Real production stakes (daily alerts breached, replica lag). From a well-known crypto data platform.
